source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Crane Incident, Nansen Spar, East Breaks 602

context.region: Gulf of America Region, East Breaks Block 602

actions_taken: Verification of Temporary Anchor Points - Lifting Authority to verify all temporary anchor points prior to lifting operations
Control of Work Update - Lifting and rigging procedures updated requiring Lifting Authority sign-off on anchor point approval
Development of HSE Alert - Alert to outline failures and corrective actions
Policy Update for Rigging and Lifting - Mandate inspections of crane whenever boom is placed into boom rest
event.summary: On 21-May-2025 at approximately 1:45 p.m., a crane incident occurred on the Nansen Spar in East Breaks 602 block. Contract scaffold builders were erecting scaffolding around the crane boom rest and used multiple 1/4-ton lever hoists as bracing, attaching them to both the scaffolding and the crane boom lattice without notifying the Lifting Authority. After a lunch break, the Crane Operator resumed lifting operations without conducting a pre-use inspection or checking for changes. When the operator lifted the boom out of the rest, one of the 1/4-ton lever hoists broke and fell approximately 30 feet to the flare boom mezzanine deck. The broken lever hoist weighed 0.5 pounds and caused minor property damage ($500). No personnel injuries were reported.
recommendations: Pre-job safety meeting should mention use of 1/4-ton lever hoist and intention to use crane boom as anchor point
Prior to crane operations resuming, a safety walk-through of crane and boom should be performed
Contract scaffold builder supervisor should notify lifting authority of intention to use crane boom as connection point
Crane operator should communicate to personnel when boom will be lifted
Toolbox talks, JSP, and JSEA should mention use of lever hoist as cantilever
Third-party personnel require training on lessee standard operating procedures
Implement requirement for pre-use inspection whenever crane boom is placed into boom rest

hazards.name: Crane Operations with Temporary Rigging Attachments

threats.name: Unauthorized attachment of lever hoists to crane boom without Lifting Authority notification
Inadequate pre-use inspection after crane shutdown and resumption of operations
Failure to communicate crane boom movement to personnel in work area
Lack of coordination between scaffold builders and crane operator
Insufficient training of third-party personnel on standard operating procedures

consequences.name: Lever hoist structural failure and breakage
Dropped object falling 30 feet to flare boom mezzanine deck
Potential personnel injury from dropped object
Property damage to equipment and structures

controls.name: Lifting Authority Approval of Rigging Attachments
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized attachment of equipment to crane boom
controls.lod_basis: Policy requirement for approval before attaching items to crane boom

supporting_text: contract scaffold builders failed to notify the Anadarko Lifting Authority of the installed equipment
According to lessee policy, permission is required before attaching any items to the crane boom

controls.name: Pre-Use Inspection of Crane Equipment
controls.barrier_role: Detect changes or damage to crane and rigging before operations resume
controls.lod_basis: Standard crane safety practice to inspect before each use

supporting_text: The Crane Operator performed a pre-use inspection at 9:00 a.m. on the morning of the incident
After the lunch break, the Crane Operator resumed lifting operations without conducting another pre-use inspection
Crane pre-use inspection - Only one-pre-use inspection is required for the day. There is no requirement to perform additional pre-use inspections if the crane is left unattended

controls.name: Communication of Crane Operations to Personnel in Work Area
controls.barrier_role: Alert personnel to crane boom movement and prevent personnel from being in danger zone
controls.lod_basis: Standard safety practice to communicate equipment movement to personnel

supporting_text: The crane operator who seen personnel working under the crane boom should have mentioned to them he was going to lift the boom out of the cradle
As soon as personnel on the deck and the scaffolding crew realized the crane boom was moving, they tried to stop the crane operation using hand signals (waving of the hands)

controls.name: Pre-Job Safety Meeting and Toolbox Talk
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all personnel understand work scope, hazards, and procedures including use of temporary rigging
controls.lod_basis: Standard safety practice to brief all personnel before work begins

supporting_text: Pre-job safety meeting with all personnel should have mentioned the use of the 1/4-ton lever hoist and the intention to use the crane boom as an anchor point
All other JSEAs and toolbox talks do not mention the use of a 1/4-ton lever hoist as a cantilever
Toolbox table talk, JSP, and JSEA's does not mention the use of a lever hoist to be used as a cantilever

controls.name: Training of Third-Party Personnel on Standard Operating Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Ensure contract personnel understand and comply with lessee procedures and safety requirements
controls.lod_basis: Requirement for third-party personnel to understand operator's standard operating procedures

supporting_text: Third party personnel are unfamiliar with lessee standard operating procedures
Insufficient knowledge and training among third-party personnel resulted in a failure to understand established standard operating procedures

controls.name: Supervision and Coordination of Simultaneous Operations
controls.barrier_role: Oversee and coordinate multiple concurrent work activities to prevent conflicts and unsafe conditions
controls.lod_basis: Requirement to manage SIMOPS in confined work areas

supporting_text: Simultaneous Operations (SIMOPS) - Contract scaffold builder, crane operations, and rigging operations were ongoing in a relatively small area
Supervision - Pre-job safety meeting with all personnel should have mentioned the use of the 1/4-ton lever hoist

controls.name: Visual Observation and Hand Signal Communication
controls.barrier_role: Detect personnel in danger zone and stop crane operations before impact
controls.lod_basis: Emergency stop procedure using hand signals to halt crane operations

supporting_text: As soon as personnel on the deck and the scaffolding crew realized the crane boom was moving, they tried to stop the crane operation using hand signals (waving of the hands), but before the Crane Operator could react, a 1/4-ton lever hoist broke

controls.name: Dropped Object Impact Assessment and Consequence Evaluation
controls.barrier_role: Assess potential consequences of dropped objects to inform risk management
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident analysis using Dropped Object Consequences calculator

supporting_text: Using this weight and the reported approximate height of 30 feet, we entered the data into a Dropped Object Consequences calculator, which indicated a slight potential consequence

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
